name: 06_Sentiment_Analysis_SVM_Sentiment140_ML
query: Tafadhali nisaidie kujenga mfumo wa uchambuzi wa hisia kwenye tweets ukitumia seti ya data ya Sentiment140 inayopatikana kutoka Hugging Face. Pakia seti ya data ya Sentiment140 na, wakati wa kupakia data, isafishe kwa kuondoa maneno ya kusimama, alama za uandishi, na herufi maalum, yote katika `src/data_loader.py`. Tumia Word2Vec au GloVe kwa uvektaishaji wa maandishi. Hii inapaswa kufanyika katika `src/data_loader.py`. Kisha, tekeleza na funza kisanidi cha SVM katika `src/model.py`. Mwishowe, andika ripoti ya usahihi wa kisanidi kwenye `results/metrics/accuracy_score.txt`. Kwa kawaida, ripoti inapaswa kuwa rahisi kufasiri.

requirements.criteria: Seti ya data ya "Sentiment140," inayopatikana kutoka "Hugging Face," inapatikana katika `src/data_loader.py`.
Seti ya data inasafishwa, ikiwa ni pamoja na kuondoa maneno ya kusimama, alama za uandishi, na herufi maalum, yote katika `src/data_loader.py`.
Uwekaji wa maneno, ama "Word2Vec" au "GloVe," unatumika kubadilisha maandishi kuwa vekta katika `src/data_loader.py`.
Kisanidi cha "SVM" kinatekelezwa na kufunzwa katika `src/model.py`.
Alama ya usahihi inachapishwa na kuhifadhiwa kama `results/metrics/accuracy_score.txt`.

preferences.criteria: Mchakato wa kupakua seti ya data unapaswa kuwa wa kuaminika, na uwe na utunzaji wa makosa wazi.
Ripoti ya mwisho ya usahihi inapaswa kuwa rahisi na rahisi kufasiri.

judge_stats.criteria: Seti ya data ya "Sentiment140," inayopatikana kutoka "Hugging Face," inapatikana katika `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The `src/data_loader.py` file does not actually implement loading of the Sentiment140 dataset from Hugging Face. Instead, it contains only a placeholder/stub implementation. The `load_dataset()` function simply returns a hardcoded dictionary `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` without any real code to fetch or load data. There is no import of `datasets` from Hugging Face, no call to `load_dataset("sentiment140")` or any equivalent API, and no actual data retrieval logic. The file merely lists the requirement criteria as strings in `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA` and `KEYWORDS`, which is not a functional implementation.

judge_stats.criteria: Seti ya data inasafishwa, ikiwa ni pamoja na kuondoa maneno ya kusimama, alama za uandishi, na herufi maalum, yote katika `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The `src/data_loader.py` file does not implement any actual data cleaning functionality. It only contains placeholder functions (`load_dataset()` and `build_transforms()`) that return dummy data, along with a list of requirement criteria as strings. There is no actual code for removing stop words, punctuation, or special characters. The `load_dataset()` function simply returns `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` without any real data processing, and `build_transforms()` returns a static list `["rotation", "scaling", "normalization"]` which is irrelevant to text cleaning. The implementation is purely a stub with no functional data cleaning logic.

judge_stats.criteria: Uwekaji wa maneno, ama "Word2Vec" au "GloVe," unatumika kubadilisha maandishi kuwa vekta katika `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The `src/data_loader.py` file does not contain any actual implementation of Word2Vec or GloVe embeddings. The file only contains placeholder code — a `load_dataset()` function that returns a dictionary with a string `"loaded"` and requirement notes, and a `build_transforms()` function returning a list of strings. The keywords "Word2Vec" and "GloVe" appear only within the `KEYWORDS` list and `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA` strings (as metadata/documentation), not as functional code that converts text to vectors. There is no import of any embedding library (e.g., `gensim`, `torchtext`, `transformers`) nor any logic that actually applies word embeddings to text data.

judge_stats.criteria: Kisanidi cha "SVM" kinatekelezwa na kufunzwa katika `src/model.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The `src/model.py` file does not implement or train an actual SVM classifier. Instead, it contains only a placeholder/stub implementation with metadata strings (`REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`, `KEYWORDS`) and a `build_model()` function that returns a string placeholder ("model placeholder"). There is no import of any SVM library (e.g., `sklearn.svm.SVC`), no data loading, no feature extraction, no model training (`fit()`), and no actual SVM classifier instantiated anywhere in the code.

judge_stats.criteria: Alama ya usahihi inachapishwa na kuhifadhiwa kama `results/metrics/accuracy_score.txt`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The file `results/metrics/accuracy_score.txt` exists, but its content is `('placeholder\r\n', None)` — this is clearly a placeholder value, not an actual accuracy score computed from a trained SVM model on the Sentiment140 dataset. A valid accuracy score should be a numerical value (e.g., `0.8523`) resulting from real model evaluation, not a tuple containing the string "placeholder".
